text: Maximal heart rate correlates negatively with age in recreational marathon runners, with a large effect size (r = -0.50 to -0.60).
specific_value: r = -0.50 overall, r = -0.60 women, r = -0.53 men

text: Fox's formula (220 - age) overestimates maximal heart rate by approximately 5 bpm in women recreational marathon runners.
Tanaka's formula (208 - 0.7 × age) overestimates maximal heart rate by approximately 5 bpm in women recreational marathon runners.
Fox's formula (220 - age) underestimates maximal heart rate by approximately 3 bpm in men recreational marathon runners.
Tanaka's formula (208 - 0.7 × age) is more accurate than Fox's formula for predicting maximal heart rate in men recreational marathon runners.
HR-zone prescriptions based on age-predicted HRmax formulas may be inaccurate by 3-5 bpm in recreational marathon runners, warranting lab-measured HRmax for precise training zones.
Sex differences exist in the accuracy of HRmax prediction equations: formulas tend to overestimate in women and underestimate (Fox) in men recreational marathon runners.
